items.text:
Subject: Field Trip Permission Form

Hi Marcus, please drop off your signed field trip permission form at the Geology Office (Room 218, Stone Hall) by Friday, May 9. Include the $12 transportation fee in cash or check. Late forms cannot be accepted.

Thanks,
Dr. Lin

questions.stem: According to the email, where must Marcus drop off the form?

options.A: At the Field Trip Welcome Center on Sunday morning
options.B: At the Geology Office in Stone Hall
options.C: At the Visitor Information Desk in Stone Hall
options.D: At Dr. Lin's mailbox in the Science Office

questions.explanation: The email tells Marcus to deliver the form to the Geology Office located in Stone Hall, Room 218.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about forms submitted after the deadline?

options.A: They are sent to the dean's office for review
options.B: They include an additional twenty-dollar penalty fee
options.C: They are processed at the end of the semester
options.D: They cannot be processed for the trip

questions.explanation: The email states late forms cannot be accepted, which implies students who miss the deadline will not be able to join the trip.

items.text:
Water Shutoff Notice

Building maintenance will shut off water in Henderson Hall on Saturday, March 21, from 7:00 AM to 1:00 PM. Bathrooms and drinking fountains will be unavailable. Bottled water will be distributed at the front desk. Please plan accordingly.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what time will the water shutoff end?
questions.explanation: The notice states the shutoff runs from 7:00 AM to 1:00 PM, so water service resumes at one in the afternoon.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students living in Henderson Hall?

options.A: They are required to move out for the entire day
options.B: They will receive a meal voucher as compensation
options.C: They should pick up bottled water on Saturday morning
options.D: They must inform the front desk of any concerns

questions.explanation: Because bottled water is being distributed at the front desk during the shutoff hours, students would benefit from collecting it on Saturday morning when bathrooms and fountains are unavailable.
